name: Christmas/Thanksgiving Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 1 poultry liver (from the bird you are using)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the chestnuts: Make a small incision on each chestnut, and roast them on the stove. If using an electric stove, place them on the burner; clean afterwards. Peel the chestnuts carefully.
2. Brown the beef with half the butter in a pan.
3. Add the rice, salt, and a small amount of water to the pan. Cook on low heat until the water is absorbed.
4. Boil the liver, mince it, and add it to the mixture of rice and meat.
5. Add the almonds, prunes, raisins, chopped chestnuts, and pine nuts. Add a small amount of water, then simmer until all the liquid is absorbed.
6. The stuffing is ready to use. Add the remaining butter and bake with the chicken in a separate pan. Remove from heat when the chicken is done.
Author's Notes:
This is an elaborate version of traditional Greek poultry stuffing. It's preferred over bread-based stuffings by those who've tried it. The chestnut-roasting instructions can be adapted; use boiling water if needed. Adjust the amounts of prunes and raisins as desired, keeping in mind that more prunes will make the stuffing sourer, while more raisins will make it sweeter. Always use pitted prunes for safety.
